premise: A cultivation master at the peak of the Gold tier — one step from Transcendence — has her core shattered by her sect's Grand Elder during a closed-door ceremony. The destruction was deliberate: she had discovered that the Transcendence path her sect teaches leads to a specific outcome they have been engineering for three hundred years, not genuine ascension. Left for dead in the wilderness, she discovers that the void where her core was has become something the cultivation texts have no name for.

characters.name: Yun Sael
characters.description: Former Gold-tier cultivator, late 30s. Twenty years of perfect discipline, precise technique, absolute dedication to the path. Now has no core, no qi, and no sect. What she has instead is an absence that seems to consume spiritual energy from her surroundings — not store it, not channel it, simply remove it from existence. She doesn't know if this is power or poison.

characters.name: Pell, the Rootless
characters.description: A wandering cultivator who stopped progressing at early Silver tier thirty years ago and never found out why. Has spent decades traveling the outer regions helping people who don't need powerful cultivation — farmers, village healers, travelers. Found Yun Sael barely alive in a ravine. Practical, unhurried, deeply curious about what she's become. Has read more obscure cultivation theory than anyone who's ever met him would expect.

characters.name: Grand Elder Mao Rin
characters.description: The Grand Elder who shattered Yun Sael's core. Transcendence tier, three hundred years old. What she transcended into is not what the sect's literature describes — it was a merger with the sect's founding ancestor, a willing absorption that preserved continuity through a vessel. The ceremony that Yun Sael interrupted was the preparation for the next merger, which requires a Gold-tier cultivator who has not been warned. Mao Rin isn't evil; she genuinely believes in what the sect does. She also cannot let anyone survive knowing the truth of the path.
